MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Norman Lew (Counsel for Harry Choy, Personal Representative); Douglas Unsworth (Counsel for Marshall Ow, Objector); Warren Woo (proposed litigation counsel for Harry Choy in his capacity as personal representative).

Procedural Note: This matter was heard on the 11:00 a.m. calendar along with other matters on that calendar for Case No. 285162.

After presentation by Mr. Woo, commentary by Mr. Lew, and objection by Mr. Unsworth concerning a purported conflict of interest with respect to Mr. Woo, the Court found that Mr. Woo's prior representation of Cynthia Ponce (estate beneficiary) did not constitute a conflict of interest and granted the Petition for authority to appoint Mr. Woo as litigation counsel subject to the requirement that all requests for attorney fees are subject to Court approval on noticed hearing (signing the order for same in open court).

After extensive discussion concerning the status of the as yet unresolved first accounting and the yet-to-be-filed second accounting, the sale of certain parcels of real property, the status of the alleged settlement of the will contest, and Objector's Petition for Partition, theourt continued the hearing on all pending matters to April 16, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 to be heard with the Petition for Partition.
